variant_label: Trade — Iraq foreign workers reduced by currency-remittance curbs
notes: Regulations curtailing the amount of money foreign workers were allowed to send out of Iraq caused about 200,000 of Iraq's 1.2 million foreign workers to leave, adversely affecting service industries and agriculture. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.8]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran oil exports, 1987 estimate under moderate Iraqi air pressure
notes: With only moderate Iraqi pressure through air attacks, Iranian oil exports in 1987 are estimated to average about 1.4 million b/d, somewhat less than in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.9]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran oil exports during intensive Iraqi air attacks, Aug-Oct 1986
notes: If Iraq carries out repeated, systematic attacks, Iranian oil exports would be kept to no more than 1 million b/d, as during the intensive air attacks of August-October 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.9]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran OPEC production quota, 1987
notes: With little or no Iraqi military pressure on Iran's oil exports/production, Iran would produce slightly above its OPEC quota of 2.25 million b/d, implying exports of 1.6-1.7 million b/d. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.9]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran implied oil export range at full OPEC-quota production
notes: Producing slightly above the 2.25 million b/d OPEC quota would imply Iranian oil exports of 1.6-1.7 million b/d absent Iraqi military pressure. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.9]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Assumed Iranian crude oil price, 1987 estimate
notes: Tehran's 1987 oil export revenue estimate assumes oil prices average $15 per barrel for Iranian crude. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.9]

Under the $15/barrel, 1.4 million b/d export scenario, Iran's 1987 imports are expected to be about 20 percent lower than 1986 as Iran maintains the deep import cuts begun mid-1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.9]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran-Iraq oil export revenue chart scenario notes
original_period_label: 1987 (scenario a: no Iraqi pressure/worst-case low prices combined in chart footnotes)
notes: Figure 6 plots Iran/Iraq oil export revenues 1978-1987 as a line chart with only gridlines (no printed per-year data labels) except for the four 1987 scenario footnotes: (a) estimated; (b) oil price of $18/barrel; (c) oil price of $15/barrel; (d) oil price of $10/barrel. Left blank per no-guessing rule -- chart values not pixel-estimated; exact 1987 scenario dollar figures are captured separately via Table 1 (Iran) and Table 3 (Iraq) current-account tables on pp.14 and 18. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Figure 6 "Iran-Iraq Oil Export Revenues, 1978-87" (line chart, billion US$; scenario years 87a/87b/87c/87d), May 1987, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Trade — Cost of Iran's oil shuttle (maintenance, charter fees, insurance)
notes: The cost of Iran's oil shuttle, which includes maintenance, charter fees, and insurance, is estimated at at least $200 million a year. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Trade — Iran nonoil exports, 1987 projected rise
notes: Iran's nonoil exports are estimated to rise by about 25 percent in 1987 but will still account for less than 15 percent of total export revenues. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Trade — Iran nonoil exports share of total export revenues, 1987
notes: Nonoil exports will still account for less than 15 percent of Iran's total export revenues in 1987 despite the projected 25 percent rise. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.10]

Slack domestic production and pressure to maintain supplies to the lower class probably will prompt Tehran to import $2.5-3 billion in food, medicines, and agricultural inputs in 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.11]

variant_label: Trade — Iran refined petroleum product imports, 1986
notes: Iran imported about $500 million in refined petroleum products in 1986, which could double in 1987 if Iraq intensifies attacks on refineries. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.11]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran oil revenue estimate, 1987 (assuming 1.4m b/d at $15/barrel)
notes: Assuming oil exports of 1.4 million b/d at an average price of $15 per barrel, Iran's 1987 oil revenues will be approximately $7.7 billion -- barely enough to cover requirements for food, fuel, and military supplies. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.12]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq oil export revenue estimate, 1987
notes: Increased oil exports and higher prices are estimated to raise Iraq's oil export revenues in 1987 to about $9.6 billion, an increase of 35 percent from 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.15]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq oil export revenue increase, 1987 vs 1986
notes: The estimated $9.6 billion in 1987 Iraqi oil export revenue represents a 35 percent increase from 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.15]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Assumed Iraqi oil export volume for the 1987 revenue estimate
notes: The 1987 Iraqi oil revenue estimate assumes Iraqi exports average 1.7-1.8 million barrels per day at $15 per barrel. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.15]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Planned expansion of Iraq's 1-million-b/d Turkey pipeline
notes: The boost in oil exports from 1986 will come from a 500,000 b/d expansion of Iraq's 1-million-b/d pipeline through Turkey, likely to be completed in fall 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.15]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Saudi restriction on Iraqi exports through the Iraqi-Saudi Spurline, Nov 1986-Feb 1987
notes: Saudi Arabia, citing technical difficulties, restricted Iraqi exports through the 500,000 b/d Iraqi-Saudi Spurline to about 200,000 b/d -- slightly less than two-thirds of the 1986 average -- between November 1986 and February 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.15]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Saudi-Kuwaiti Neutral Zone oil sold on Iraq's behalf
notes: Under a 1983 agreement, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait sell about 240,000 b/d of oil from the Saudi-Kuwaiti Neutral Zone and give the proceeds to Iraq monthly (though 1986 first-half sales fell below the contracted amount). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Value of Neutral Zone oil aid to Iraq, 1986
notes: Neutral Zone oil sales on Iraq's behalf totaled about $800 million last year (1986). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Saudi Arabia's own-oil sales on Iraq's behalf, average volume
notes: Saudi Arabia sold an average of 60,000 b/d of its own oil for Iraq last year, totaling about $250 million -- almost all of which was provided to the Soviet Union to pay for Baghdad's military purchases. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Value of Saudi own-oil sales on Iraq's behalf, 1986
notes: Saudi Arabia's own-oil sales on Iraq's behalf totaled about $250 million in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Oil sales/cash-equivalent aid on Iraq's behalf, present level
notes: Oil sales on Iraq's behalf, or their cash equivalent, probably will continue at their present level of about 300,000 b/d at least until the second Iraq-Turkey pipeline starts operation. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

Iraq is estimated to boost its nonoil exports by one-third in 1987 to about $600 million, mostly from higher natural gas sales to Kuwait. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

Iraq's 1986 nonoil exports -- predominantly dates, fruits and vegetables, natural gas, and industrial products -- totaled nearly $450 million, about 6 percent of total export revenues. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Trade — Iraq nonoil exports share of total export revenues, 1986
notes: Iraq's 1986 nonoil exports represented about 6 percent of total export revenues. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq natural gas exports to Kuwait via new pipeline
notes: Baghdad is exporting 200 million cubic feet per day of natural gas to Kuwait through a new pipeline (financed by Kuwait) from the Ar Rumaylah oilfield to Kuwait's Shuaiba industrial area; Baghdad plans to double this and begin exporting about 40,000 b/d of petroleum products through a parallel line in 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq planned petroleum-product exports via new parallel pipeline to Kuwait
notes: Baghdad plans this year to begin exporting about 40,000 b/d of petroleum products through a parallel line to Kuwait, alongside doubling natural gas exports. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq OPEC-assigned quota rejected by Baghdad, Dec 1986

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq's demanded OPEC quota (claimed parity with Iran)
notes: Baghdad's insistence on a quota equal to Iran's -- 2.25 million b/d -- has drawn increasing criticism from other OPEC members. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.25]

variant_label: Trade — Decline in Japan/W.Germany/UK/Italy/France exports to Iran+Iraq, Q3 1986 vs Q3 1985
notes: Import cuts imposed by Iran and Iraq cut exports from Japan, West Germany, the UK, Italy, and France (together about half of all Iran+Iraq imports in 1985) by about 30 percent in Q3 1986 versus Q3 1985.
